title: Brooke Rollins, Secretary of Agriculture | All-In DC
description:
(0:00) David Friedberg welcomes Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins!

(1:32) How she ended up in Trump's two administrations, history of the USDA

(16:56) Trump's first 100 days, what needs to be reformed in the USDA

(21:31) SNAP's massive impact on USDA budget: soda, obesity, health challenges; how being DC outsiders helps the Trump Administration

(30:48) How she reconciles running a department with a lot of government intervention after coming from a free market background

(34:33) State of US farming in 2025, labor costs, opening up new markets, and more

(42:11) Working with DOGE, Farm Bill negotiation, stance on agricultural innovation

analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast features an interview with Brooke Rollins, the Secretary of Agriculture, discussing her background, the USDA, and agricultural innovation. They also touch on the SNAP program and its impact on the USDA budget. The conversation covers various aspects of US farming, including labor costs and market expansion.

takeaways.text: Free market background influences USDA decisions
New markets can boost US farming

sentiment.notes: The conversation is informative and solution-focused, with a positive tone towards agricultural innovation and reform.

notable_moments.description: SNAP program's impact on USDA budget
